title: Caramelized Salmon with Chinese Hash
description: A flavorful and nutritious Chinese-inspired dish featuring pan-seared salmon with a sweet and savory caramelized sauce served atop a crispy hash.
instructions: Season the salmon fillets with salt and pepper.
Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the salmon and cook for 2-3 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and cook for an additional 4-6 minutes, or until cooked to your desired level of doneness.
Meanwhile, heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a small skillet over medium heat. Add the hash and cook until crispy and golden brown.

In a small saucepan, combine the sherry, soy sauce, brown sugar, ginger, and garlic. Bring to a boil over medium heat, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 2-3 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened slightly.
To serve, place a salmon fillet on each plate, then top with a spoonful of the caramelized sauce and a sprinkle of chopped green onions. Serve with a side of crispy hash.
